$schema_note: Iter-9 backfill snapshot fixture (see us-ecommerce-pmi-2026q1.json note). Source-of-record for wc inputs cited by domestic ecommerce profiles. The profile's source.ref points here; profile inputs MIRROR this file's data block.
applies_to_note: US ecommerce. V1 inline WC base (30bps) carried forward; peak bumped to 60bps (illustrative) for the higher inventory-financing intensity of ecommerce vs services.

source.citation: V1 inline WC base (30bps) carried forward; peak 60bps illustrative.
source.retrieval_method: carried from V1 / fabricated for the Iter-7 composer test. STAYS snapshot in Iter-9: wc_trapezoidal inputs are output bps, not a market observable, so there is no input to bind.
source.live_binding_target: NONE via direct bind. A derivation from FRED ISRATIO into wc bps is out of Iter-9 scope (flagged: profile annotations elsewhere label it Iter-9 work, conflicting with the carried scope decision).
